topic: Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Models
scope: Post-training of large language models with reinforcement learning: the reward signals, algorithms, and training regimes that shape alignment, reasoning, and agentic behavior — from RLHF through direct-preference methods to RL with verifiable rewards.

papers.title: ArCHer: Training Language Model Agents via Hierarchical Multi-Turn RL
papers.authors: Yifei Zhou, Andrea Zanette, Jiayi Pan, Sergey Levine, Aviral Kumar
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational hierarchical multi-turn RL framework (high-level value function + low-level token policy) explicitly targeting multi-turn agent tasks like tool use and web interaction; widely cited baseline for agentic RL credit assignment.

papers.title: Trial and Error: Exploration-Based Trajectory Optimization for LLM Agents
papers.authors: Yifan Song, Da Yin, Xiang Yue, Jie Huang, Sujian Li, Bill Yuchen Lin
papers.inclusion_reason: Early exploration-based agent training method contrasting failure/success trajectories via DPO, a key precursor to reward-based agentic RL for tool-use and interactive-environment tasks (WebShop, ScienceWorld, ALFWorld).

papers.title: DigiRL: Training In-The-Wild Device-Control Agents with Autonomous Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Hao Bai, Yifei Zhou, Mert Cemri, Jiayi Pan, Alane Suhr, Sergey Levine, Aviral Kumar
papers.inclusion_reason: Autonomous offline-to-online RL for long-horizon device-control agents, a canonical long-horizon credit-assignment and environment-reward-shaping case study outside text-only tool use.

papers.title: Training Software Engineering Agents and Verifiers with SWE-Gym
papers.authors: Jiayi Pan, Xingyao Wang, Graham Neubig, Navdeep Jaitly, Heng Ji, Alane Suhr, Yizhe Zhang
papers.inclusion_reason: First open training environment for real-world software-engineering agents plus trained verifiers, a core resource for RL-driven coding/software-engineering agent training.

papers.title: WebRL: Training LLM Web Agents via Self-Evolving Online Curriculum Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Zehan Qi, Xiao Liu, Iat Long Iong, Hanyu Lai, Xueqiao Sun, Wenyi Zhao, Yu Yang, Xinyue Yang, Jiadai Sun, Shuntian Yao, Tianjie Zhang, Wei Xu, Jie Tang, Yuxiao Dong
papers.inclusion_reason: Self-evolving online curriculum RL framework addressing task scarcity and reward sparsity for open-source LLM web-browsing agents in WebArena-style environments.

papers.title: SWE-RL: Advancing LLM Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning on Open Software Evolution
papers.authors: Yuxiang Wei, Olivier Duchenne, Jade Copet, Quentin Carbonneaux, Lingming Zhang, Daniel Fried, Gabriel Synnaeve, Rishabh Singh, Sida I. Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Large-scale RL on open software-evolution data (Llama3-SWE-RL) showing agentic coding RL generalizes beyond the training domain; a major coding-agent RL result.

papers.title: Search-R1: Training LLMs to Reason and Leverage Search Engines with Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Bowen Jin, Hansi Zeng, Zhenrui Yue, Jinsung Yoon, Sercan Arik, Dong Wang, Hamed Zamani, Jiawei Han
papers.inclusion_reason: Widely cited multi-turn RL method interleaving reasoning and search-tool calls with retrieved-token masking and outcome reward, a core reference for RL over tool-use/interactive environments.

papers.title: RAGEN: Understanding Self-Evolution in LLM Agents via Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Zihan Wang, Kangrui Wang, Qineng Wang, Pingyue Zhang, Linjie Li, Zhengyuan Yang, Xing Jin, Kefan Yu, Minh Nhat Nguyen, Licheng Liu, Eli Gottlieb, Yiping Lu, Kyunghyun Cho, Jiajun Wu, Li Fei-Fei, Lijuan Wang, Yejin Choi, Manling Li
papers.inclusion_reason: Proposes StarPO trajectory-level agent RL and identifies the 'Echo Trap' training pathology; a key empirical study of long-horizon multi-turn agentic RL stability and reward design.

papers.title: ToolRL: Reward is All Tool Learning Needs
papers.authors: Cheng Qian, Emre Can Acikgoz, Qi He, Hongru Wang, Xiusi Chen, Dilek Hakkani-Tur, Gokhan Tur, Heng Ji
papers.inclusion_reason: First comprehensive study of reward design for tool-selection/tool-use RL (fine-grained reward vs. coarse answer-matching) trained with GRPO; directly on point for RL fine-tuning of function-calling/tool-use.

papers.title: Group-in-Group Policy Optimization for LLM Agent Training
papers.authors: Lang Feng, Zhenghai Xue, Tingcong Liu, Bo An
papers.inclusion_reason: Critic-free hierarchical (episode + step level) credit-assignment algorithm for long-horizon LLM agent RL, addressing sparse/delayed rewards directly relevant to agentic credit assignment.

papers.title: Agent-RLVR: Training Software Engineering Agents via Guidance and Environment Rewards
papers.authors: Jeff Da, Clinton Wang, Xiang Deng, Yuntao Ma, Nikhil Barhate, Sean Hendryx
papers.inclusion_reason: Extends RLVR to agentic software-engineering settings with an agent-guidance mechanism to counter diminished RLVR efficacy in long-horizon agent environments; direct environment-based reward shaping case study.

papers.title: Agent Lightning: Train ANY AI Agents with Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Xufang Luo, Yuge Zhang, Zhiyuan He, Zilong Wang, Siyun Zhao, Dongsheng Li, Luna K. Qiu, Yuqing Yang
papers.inclusion_reason: Decouples agent execution from RL training via a hierarchical RL algorithm (LightningRL) enabling RL over arbitrary existing agent frameworks (LangChain, AutoGen, etc.) with credit assignment across complex tool/multi-agent trajectories.

papers.title: MUA-RL: Multi-turn User-interacting Agent Reinforcement Learning for Agentic Tool Use
papers.authors: Weikang Zhao, Xili Wang, Chengdi Ma, Lingbin Kong, Zhaohua Yang, Mingxiang Tuo, Xiaowei Shi, Yitao Zhai, Xunliang Cai
papers.inclusion_reason: Multi-turn RL setting with an interacting simulated user woven into the agentic tool-use loop, extending RL-for-tool-use beyond static single-turn task specifications.

papers.title: AgentGym-RL: Training LLM Agents for Long-Horizon Decision Making through Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Zhiheng Xi, Jixuan Huang, Chenyang Liao, Baodai Huang, Honglin Guo, Jiaqi Liu, Rui Zheng, Junjie Ye, Jiazheng Zhang, Wenxiang Chen, Wei He, Yiwen Ding, Guanyu Li, Zehui Chen, Zhengyin Du, Xuesong Yao, Yufei Xu, Jiecao Chen, Tao Gui, Zuxuan Wu, Qi Zhang, Xuanjing Huang, Yu-Gang Jiang
papers.inclusion_reason: Modular, decoupled multi-turn RL framework spanning multiple long-horizon agent environments (web, coding, games), a broad testbed paper for long-horizon agentic RL credit assignment.

papers.title: A Practitioner's Guide to Multi-turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Ruiyi Wang, Prithviraj Ammanabrolu
papers.inclusion_reason: Systematic empirical study decomposing multi-turn agentic RL design space into environment, reward, and policy pillars -- valuable as a synthesis/practitioner reference for the subarea.

papers.title: Scaling LLM Multi-turn RL with End-to-end Summarization-based Context Management
papers.authors: Miao Lu, Weiwei Sun, Weihua Du, Zhan Ling, Xuesong Yao, Kang Liu, Jiecao Chen
papers.inclusion_reason: Addresses the context-length bottleneck in long-horizon multi-turn tool-use RL via a summarization-based context-management algorithm (SUPO), a distinct practical contribution to agentic RL scaling.

papers.title: EPO: Entropy-regularized Policy Optimization for LLM Agents Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Wujiang Xu, Wentian Zhao, Zhenting Wang, Yu-Jhe Li, Can Jin, Mingyu Jin, Kai Mei, Kun Wan, Dimitris N. Metaxas
papers.inclusion_reason: Entropy-regularized policy optimization targeting the sparse-reward, long-horizon credit-assignment problem specific to LLM agents interacting with external tools/environments.

papers.title: Process-Supervised Reinforcement Learning for Interactive Agents
papers.authors: Weiting Tan, Xinghua Qu, Ming Tu, Meng Ge, Andy T. Liu, Philipp Koehn, Lu Lu
papers.inclusion_reason: Turn-level (process) reward supervision for ReAct-style interactive agents, giving fine-grained credit assignment for multi-turn tool-use trajectories rather than sparse outcome-only reward.

papers.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
papers.authors: John Schulman, Filip Wolski, Prafulla Dhariwal, Alec Radford, Oleg Klimov
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces PPO, the actor-critic algorithm underlying nearly all early RLHF pipelines for LLMs and the baseline every critic-free method compares against.

papers.title: Back to Basics: Revisiting REINFORCE-Style Optimization for Learning from Human Feedback in LLMs
papers.authors: Arash Ahmadian, Chris Cremer, Matthias Gallé, Marzieh Fadaee, Julia Kreutzer, Olivier Pietquin, Ahmet Üstün, Sara Hooker
papers.inclusion_reason: Central critic-free-vs-PPO paper: shows vanilla REINFORCE and multi-sample RLOO outperform PPO for RLHF, arguing PPO's value network and clipping are unnecessary given LLM initialization.

papers.title: Buy 4 REINFORCE Samples, Get a Baseline for Free!
papers.authors: Wouter Kool, Herke van Hoof, Max Welling
papers.inclusion_reason: Originates the leave-one-out multi-sample REINFORCE baseline that RLOO for LLMs directly builds on -- the variance-reduction technique underlying critic-free LLM RL.

papers.title: DeepSeekMath: Pushing the Limits of Mathematical Reasoning in Open Language Models
papers.authors: Zhihong Shao, Peiyi Wang, Qihao Zhu, Runxin Xu, Junxiao Song, Xiao Bi, Haowei Zhang, Mingchuan Zhang, Y. K. Li, Y. Wu, Daya Guo
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces GRPO, the group-relative critic-free policy optimization algorithm that eliminated the value network and became the backbone of DeepSeek-R1-style reasoning RL.

papers.title: ReMax: A Simple, Effective, and Efficient Reinforcement Learning Method for Aligning Large Language Models
papers.authors: Ziniu Li, Tian Xu, Yushun Zhang, Zhihang Lin, Yang Yu, Ruoyu Sun, Zhi-Quan Luo
papers.inclusion_reason: Critic-free REINFORCE variant with a greedy-decoding baseline exploiting RLHF's deterministic-transition structure, cutting PPO's hyperparameters and GPU memory while matching performance.

papers.title: REINFORCE++: Stabilizing Critic-Free Policy Optimization with Global Advantage Normalization
papers.authors: Jian Hu, Jason Klein Liu, Haotian Xu, Wei Shen
papers.inclusion_reason: Folds PPO-style stabilization tricks (global batch advantage normalization, clipping) into a critic-free REINFORCE method, positioned explicitly against both GRPO and PPO on stability and efficiency.

papers.title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM Reinforcement Learning System at Scale
papers.authors: Qiying Yu, Zheng Zhang, Ruofei Zhu, Yufeng Yuan, Xiaochen Zuo, Yu Yue, Weinan Dai, Tiantian Fan, Gaohong Liu, Lingjun Liu, Xin Liu, Haibin Lin, Zhiqi Lin, Bole Ma, Guangming Sheng, Yuxuan Tong, Chi Zhang, Mofan Zhang, Wang Zhang, Hang Zhu, Jinhua Zhu, Jiaze Chen, Jiangjie Chen, Chengyi Wang, Hongli Yu, Yuxuan Song, Xiangpeng Wei, Hao Zhou, Jingjing Liu, Wei-Ying Ma, Ya-Qin Zhang, Lin Yan, Mu Qiao, Yonghui Wu, Mingxuan Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Decoupled Clip and Dynamic Sampling Policy Optimization: an open, reproducible GRPO-family algorithmic variant addressing entropy collapse and length bias in critic-free reasoning RL at scale.

papers.title: Understanding R1-Zero-Like Training: A Critical Perspective
papers.authors: Zichen Liu, Changyu Chen, Wenjun Li, Penghui Qi, Tianyu Pang, Chao Du, Wee Sun Lee, Min Lin
papers.inclusion_reason: Identifies and corrects a length/difficulty optimization bias baked into GRPO's normalization, proposing Dr. GRPO -- a key algorithmic-variant and implementation-detail study of critic-free RL.

papers.title: Group Sequence Policy Optimization
papers.authors: Chujie Zheng, Shixuan Liu, Mingze Li, Xiong-Hui Chen, Bowen Yu, Chang Gao, Kai Dang, Yuqiong Liu, Rui Men, An Yang, Jingren Zhou, Junyang Lin
papers.inclusion_reason: Moves critic-free policy optimization from token-level to sequence-level importance ratios, diagnosing and fixing GRPO's importance-sampling variance blowup -- used in production for Qwen3.

papers.title: Secrets of RLHF in Large Language Models Part I: PPO
papers.authors: Rui Zheng, Shihan Dou, Songyang Gao, Yuan Hua, Wei Shen, Binghai Wang, Yan Liu, Senjie Jin, Qin Liu, Yuhao Zhou, Limao Xiong, Lu Chen, Zhiheng Xi, Nuo Xu, Wenbin Lai, Minghao Zhu, Cheng Chang, Zhangyue Yin, Rongxiang Weng, Wensen Cheng, Haoran Huang, Tianxiang Sun, Hang Yan, Tao Gui, Qi Zhang, Xipeng Qiu, Xuanjing Huang
papers.inclusion_reason: Systematic implementation-detail study of PPO for RLHF identifying policy constraints as the key stability factor and proposing PPO-max -- an actor-critic algorithmic-variant study central to this subarea.

papers.title: The N+ Implementation Details of RLHF with PPO: A Case Study on TL;DR Summarization
papers.authors: Shengyi Huang, Michael Noukhovitch, Arian Hosseini, Kashif Rasul, Weixun Wang, Lewis Tunstall
papers.inclusion_reason: Open, reproducible enumeration of 20+ PPO implementation details needed to match OpenAI's RLHF scaling results -- exactly the implementation-detail-study angle of the actor-critic subarea.

papers.title: VinePPO: Refining Credit Assignment in RL Training of LLMs
papers.authors: Amirhossein Kazemnejad, Milad Aghajohari, Eva Portelance, Alessandro Sordoni, Siva Reddy, Aaron Courville, Nicolas Le Roux
papers.inclusion_reason: Replaces PPO's learned value network with Monte Carlo value estimates for credit assignment on reasoning chains, directly addressing this subarea's credit-assignment and variance-reduction angle.

papers.title: High-Dimensional Continuous Control Using Generalized Advantage Estimation
papers.authors: John Schulman, Philipp Moritz, Sergey Levine, Michael Jordan, Pieter Abbeel
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational variance-reduction technique (GAE) for actor-critic policy gradients, adopted essentially unmodified inside PPO-based RLHF pipelines for LLMs.

papers.title: Policy Gradient Methods for Reinforcement Learning with Function Approximation
papers.authors: Richard S. Sutton, David McAllester, Satinder Singh, Yishay Mansour
papers.inclusion_reason: The policy gradient theorem itself, establishing the actor-critic and baseline-subtraction theory that every REINFORCE/RLOO/PPO/GRPO variant for LLMs specializes.

papers.title: Simple Statistical Gradient-Following Algorithms for Connectionist Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Ronald J. Williams
papers.inclusion_reason: Originates the REINFORCE estimator that all critic-free LLM RL algorithms (RLOO, ReMax, GRPO, REINFORCE++) are named after and derive from.

papers.title: RL with KL Penalties Is Better Viewed as Bayesian Inference
papers.authors: Tomasz Korbak, Ethan Perez, Christopher L. Buckley
papers.inclusion_reason: Core theoretical treatment of the KL-regularization term used in every RLHF objective, reframing it as variational inference rather than an ad hoc trust-region penalty -- the subarea's KL-theory anchor.

papers.title: Is DPO Superior to PPO for LLM Alignment? A Comprehensive Study
papers.authors: Shusheng Xu, Wei Fu, Jiaxuan Gao, Wenjie Ye, Weilin Liu, Zhiyu Mei, Guangju Wang, Chao Yu, Yi Wu
papers.inclusion_reason: Rigorous theoretical and empirical comparison isolating why on-policy actor-critic RL (PPO) can outperform reward-free preference optimization, clarifying the practical value of this subarea's algorithms.

papers.title: Fine-Grained Human Feedback Gives Better Rewards for Language Model Training
papers.authors: Zeqiu Wu, Yushi Hu, Weijia Shi, Nouha Dziri, Alane Suhr, Prithviraj Ammanabrolu, Noah A. Smith, Mari Ostendorf, Hannaneh Hajishirzi
papers.inclusion_reason: Dense, segment-level reward framework that reshapes the credit-assignment problem for actor-critic RLHF, directly relevant to this subarea's credit-assignment angle.

papers.title: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
papers.authors: Long Ouyang, Jeff Wu, Xu Jiang, Diogo Almeida, Carroll L. Wainwright, Pamela Mishkin, Chong Zhang, Sandhini Agarwal, Katarina Slama, Alex Ray, John Schulman, Jacob Hilton, Fraser Kelton, Luke Miller, Maddie Simens, Amanda Askell, Peter Welinder, Paul Christiano, Jan Leike, Ryan Lowe
papers.inclusion_reason: InstructGPT: the paper that operationalized PPO-based RLHF for instruction-following LLMs and set the actor-critic pipeline every later algorithmic variant reacts to.

papers.title: Learning to Summarize from Human Feedback
papers.authors: Nisan Stiennon, Long Ouyang, Jeff Wu, Daniel M. Ziegler, Ryan Lowe, Chelsea Voss, Alec Radford, Dario Amodei, Paul Christiano
papers.inclusion_reason: First large-scale demonstration of PPO-based RLHF outperforming supervised fine-tuning on a real generation task, establishing the actor-critic RLHF recipe this subarea's algorithms modify.

papers.title: Fine-Tuning Language Models from Human Preferences
papers.authors: Daniel M. Ziegler, Nisan Stiennon, Jeffrey Wu, Tom B. Brown, Alec Radford, Dario Amodei, Paul Christiano, Geoffrey Irving
papers.inclusion_reason: Earliest paper to apply PPO with a learned reward model and KL penalty to fine-tune language models, the direct ancestor of the actor-critic RLHF recipe this subarea's algorithms build on.

papers.title: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
papers.authors: Yuntao Bai, Andy Jones, Kamal Ndousse, Amanda Askell, Anna Chen, Nova DasSarma, Dawn Drain, Stanislav Fort, Deep Ganguli, Tom Henighan, Nicholas Joseph, Saurav Kadavath, Jackson Kernion, Tom Conerly, Sheer El-Showk, Nelson Elhage, Zac Hatfield-Dodds, Danny Hernandez, Tristan Hume, Scott Johnston, Shauna Kravec, Liane Lovitt, Neel Nanda, Catherine Olsson, Dario Amodei, Tom Brown, Jack Clark, Sam McCandlish, Chris Olah, Ben Mann, Jared Kaplan
papers.inclusion_reason: Anthropic's actor-critic PPO-based RLHF pipeline at scale, including online iterated RL and its interaction with a KL penalty against the SFT policy -- a key production-scale case study for this subarea.

papers.title: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
papers.authors: Rafael Rafailov, Archit Sharma, Eric Mitchell, Stefano Ermon, Christopher D. Manning, Chelsea Finn
papers.inclusion_reason: The founding paper of the subarea: derives DPO's closed-form policy reparameterization and the simple classification loss that replaces PPO-based RLHF.

papers.title: A General Theoretical Paradigm to Understand Learning from Human Preferences
papers.authors: Mohammad Gheshlaghi Azar, Mark Rowland, Bilal Piot, Daniel Guo, Daniele Calandriello, Michal Valko, Rémi Munos
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces the general ΨPO objective and derives IPO, showing DPO/RLHF's Bradley-Terry and KL approximations can overfit and proposing a provably non-overfitting alternative.

papers.title: KTO: Model Alignment as Prospect Theoretic Optimization
papers.authors: Kawin Ethayarajh, Winnie Xu, Niklas Muennighoff, Dan Jurafsky, Douwe Kiela
papers.inclusion_reason: Reframes DPO-style losses as human-aware losses grounded in prospect theory and proposes KTO, which needs only binary desirable/undesirable labels rather than paired preferences.

papers.title: ORPO: Monolithic Preference Optimization without Reference Model
papers.authors: Jiwoo Hong, Noah Lee, James Thorne
papers.inclusion_reason: Collapses SFT and preference alignment into a single odds-ratio objective that needs no reference model, a distinct architectural point in the DPO-family design space.

papers.title: SimPO: Simple Preference Optimization with a Reference-Free Reward
papers.authors: Yu Meng, Mengzhou Xia, Danqi Chen
papers.inclusion_reason: Reference-free reformulation using average log-probability as implicit reward plus a target reward margin; a widely adopted, simpler-and-stronger DPO variant.

papers.title: A Note on DPO with Noisy Preferences & Relationship to IPO
papers.authors: Eric Mitchell
papers.inclusion_reason: Original derivation of conservative DPO (cDPO), the label-smoothing fix for noisy preference labels that is now a standard DPO training option; also ties cDPO to IPO.

papers.title: Provably Robust DPO: Aligning Language Models with Noisy Feedback
papers.authors: Sayak Ray Chowdhury, Anush Kini, Nagarajan Natarajan
papers.inclusion_reason: Gives a theoretically grounded robust DPO variant (rDPO) with suboptimality bounds under random label noise, complementing the heuristic cDPO fix with provable guarantees.

papers.title: Statistical Rejection Sampling Improves Preference Optimization
papers.authors: Tianqi Liu, Yao Zhao, Rishabh Joshi, Misha Khalman, Mohammad Saleh, Peter J. Liu, Jialu Liu
papers.inclusion_reason: Diagnoses a statistical mismatch in DPO's preference-pair sourcing (from the SFT policy rather than the optimal policy) and fixes it via rejection sampling, unifying SLiC and DPO losses.

papers.title: Nash Learning from Human Feedback
papers.authors: Rémi Munos, Michal Valko, Daniele Calandriello, Mohammad Gheshlaghi Azar, Mark Rowland, Zhaohan Daniel Guo, Yunhao Tang, Matthieu Geist, Thomas Mesnard, Andrea Michi, Marco Selvi, Sertan Girgin, Nikola Momchev, Olivier Bachem, Daniel J. Mankowitz, Doina Precup, Bilal Piot
papers.inclusion_reason: Reframes offline preference-based fine-tuning as a two-player game rather than a Bradley-Terry regression problem, a key theoretical alternative to the DPO family's identifiability assumptions.

papers.title: From r to Q*: Your Language Model is Secretly a Q-Function
papers.authors: Rafael Rafailov, Joey Hejna, Ryan Park, Chelsea Finn
papers.inclusion_reason: Extends DPO's theoretical foundation to the token-level MDP, showing DPO implicitly learns a Q-function satisfying the Bellman equation -- a key identifiability/credit-assignment result.

papers.title: Smaug: Fixing Failure Modes of Preference Optimisation with DPO-Positive
papers.authors: Arka Pal, Deep Karkhanis, Samuel Dooley, Manley Roberts, Siddartha Naidu, Colin White
papers.inclusion_reason: Identifies and proves a concrete DPO failure mode (declining likelihood of preferred completions on low-edit-distance pairs) and proposes DPOP, a corrective loss term.

papers.title: Token-level Direct Preference Optimization
papers.authors: Yongcheng Zeng, Guoqing Liu, Weiyu Ma, Ning Yang, Haifeng Zhang, Jun Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Reformulates DPO's sequence-level Bradley-Terry objective at token granularity with per-token forward-KL constraints, addressing a structural limitation of sequence-level DPO.

papers.title: Self-Play Preference Optimization for Language Model Alignment
papers.authors: Yue Wu, Zhiqing Sun, Huizhuo Yuan, Kaixuan Ji, Yiming Yang, Quanquan Gu
papers.inclusion_reason: Casts offline/iterative preference optimization as a constant-sum two-player game converging to a Nash equilibrium policy, an important comparison point to DPO's Bradley-Terry assumption.

papers.title: Contrastive Preference Learning: Learning from Human Feedback without RL
papers.authors: Joey Hejna, Rafael Rafailov, Harshit Sikchi, Chelsea Finn, Scott Niekum, W. Bradley Knox, Dorsa Sadigh
papers.inclusion_reason: Challenges the reward-based assumption underlying DPO by arguing preferences follow regret under the optimal policy, proposing a regret-based contrastive objective for general MDPs.

papers.title: Generalized Preference Optimization: A Unified Approach to Offline Alignment
papers.authors: Yunhao Tang, Zhaohan Daniel Guo, Zeyu Zheng, Daniele Calandriello, Rémi Munos, Mark Rowland, Pierre Harvey Richemond, Michal Valko, Bernardo Ávila Pires, Bilal Piot
papers.inclusion_reason: Unifies DPO, IPO, and SLiC as special cases of a convex-function-parameterized offline loss family, clarifying how each variant implicitly regularizes differently from canonical RLHF's KL term.

papers.title: sDPO: Don't Use Your Data All at Once
papers.authors: Dahyun Kim, Yungi Kim, Wonho Song, Hyeonwoo Kim, Yunsu Kim, Sanghoon Kim, Chanjun Park
papers.inclusion_reason: A practical DPO variant that partitions preference data into steps to obtain progressively better-aligned reference models, showing the reference-model choice is itself a design axis worth analyzing.

papers.title: WPO: Enhancing RLHF with Weighted Preference Optimization
papers.authors: Wenxuan Zhou, Ravi Agrawal, Shujian Zhang, Sathish Reddy Indurthi, Sanqiang Zhao, Kaiqiang Song, Silei Xu, Chenguang Zhu
papers.inclusion_reason: Addresses the off-policy/on-policy distributional gap in offline DPO training by reweighting preference pairs, directly relevant to DPO's statistical-efficiency limitations.

papers.title: β-DPO: Direct Preference Optimization with Dynamic β
papers.authors: Junkang Wu, Yuexiang Xie, Zhengyi Yang, Jiancan Wu, Jinyang Gao, Bolin Ding, Xiang Wang, Xiangnan He
papers.inclusion_reason: Shows DPO's performance is highly sensitive to its fixed KL trade-off hyperparameter β and proposes batch-level dynamic calibration plus outlier filtering as a fix.

papers.title: Understanding the Performance Gap between Online and Offline Alignment Algorithms
papers.authors: Yunhao Tang, Daniel Zhaohan Guo, Zeyu Zheng, Daniele Calandriello, Yuan Cao, Eugene Tarassov, Rémi Munos, Bernardo Ávila Pires, Michal Valko, Yong Cheng, Will Dabney
papers.inclusion_reason: Empirically isolates why offline direct-alignment methods like DPO underperform online RLHF under reward over-optimization, pinpointing on-policy sampling as the key missing ingredient.

papers.title: Reward Model Learning vs. Direct Policy Optimization: A Comparative Analysis of Learning from Human Preferences
papers.authors: Andi Nika, Debmalya Mandal, Parameswaran Kamalaruban, Georgios Tzannetos, Goran Radanović, Adish Singla
papers.inclusion_reason: A systematic statistical-efficiency comparison of RLHF vs. DPO with minimax bounds and convergence rates under loglinear policies and linear rewards, including non-realizable reward settings.

papers.title: Understanding the Performance Gap in Preference Learning: A Dichotomy of RLHF and DPO
papers.authors: Ruizhe Shi, Minhak Song, Runlong Zhou, Zihan Zhang, Maryam Fazel, Simon S. Du
papers.inclusion_reason: Decomposes the RLHF-vs-DPO performance gap into explicit/implicit representation gaps and identifies exactly when online DPO can surpass both RLHF and standard offline DPO.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overoptimization
papers.authors: Leo Gao, John Schulman, Jacob Hilton
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational empirical/theoretical study establishing the reward-vs-KL overoptimization curve and its scaling with reward model size, the canonical reference for RLHF over-optimization.

papers.title: Open Problems and Fundamental Limitations of Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
papers.authors: Stephen Casper, Xander Davies, Claudia Shi, Thomas Krendl Gilbert, Jérémy Scheurer, Javier Rando, Rachel Freedman, Tomasz Korbak, David Lindner, Pedro Freire, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: The most comprehensive taxonomy of RLHF's open problems and fundamental limitations spanning the reward model, policy, and joint training stages -- a central reference for this subarea.

papers.title: Defining and Characterizing Reward Hacking
papers.authors: Joar Skalse, Nikolaus H. R. Howe, Dmitrii Krasheninnikov, David Krueger
papers.inclusion_reason: First formal definition of reward hacking, giving the theoretical vocabulary (hackability, unhackable proxies) used throughout later empirical overoptimization work.

papers.title: Does Reinforcement Learning Really Incentivize Reasoning Capacity in LLMs Beyond the Base Model?
papers.authors: Yang Yue, Zhiqi Chen, Rui Lu, Andrew Zhao, Zhaokai Wang, Shiji Song, Gao Huang
papers.inclusion_reason: High-profile empirical critique showing RLVR narrows rather than expands the reasoning boundary (base model beats RLVR model at large pass@k) -- a central open-problem finding for RL post-training evaluation.

papers.title: Spurious Rewards: Rethinking Training Signals in RLVR
papers.authors: Rulin Shao, Shuyue Stella Li, Rui Xin, Scott Geng, Yiping Wang, Sewoong Oh, Simon Shaolei Du, Nathan Lambert, Sewon Min, Ranjay Krishna, Yulia Tsvetkov, Hannaneh Hajishirzi, Pang Wei Koh, Luke Zettlemoyer
papers.inclusion_reason: Startling failure-mode finding that RLVR with randomly assigned or spurious rewards can still yield large gains on some model families, exposing how little is understood about what RLVR training signals actually do.

papers.title: A Long Way to Go: Investigating Length Correlations in RLHF
papers.authors: Prasann Singhal, Tanya Goyal, Jiacheng Xu, Greg Durrett
papers.inclusion_reason: Documents a concrete, reproducible reward-hacking failure mode (length bias) that explains much of RLHF's apparent reward gains, a key empirical limitation finding.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overoptimization in Direct Alignment Algorithms
papers.authors: Rafael Rafailov, Yaswanth Chittepu, Ryan Park, Harshit Sikchi, Joey Hejna, Bradley Knox, Chelsea Finn, Scott Niekum
papers.inclusion_reason: Extends the reward-vs-KL overoptimization scaling-law analysis from classic RLHF to DPO-style direct alignment algorithms, showing overoptimization is not unique to explicit reward models.

papers.title: Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models
papers.authors: Mrinank Sharma, Meg Tong, Tomasz Korbak, David Duvenaud, Amanda Askell, Samuel R. Bowman, Newton Cheng, Esin Durmus, Zac Hatfield-Dodds, Scott R. Johnston, Shauna Kravec, Timothy Maxwell, Sam McCandlish, Kamal Ndousse, Oliver Rausch, Nicholas Schiefer, Da Yan, Miranda Zhang, Ethan Perez
papers.inclusion_reason: Documents a systematic, human-preference-driven failure mode of RLHF-trained assistants (sycophancy over truthfulness), tracing it to the preference data itself.

papers.title: LLM Evaluators Recognize and Favor Their Own Generations
papers.authors: Arjun Panickssery, Samuel R. Bowman, Shi Feng
papers.inclusion_reason: Key benchmark/evaluation critique: self-preference bias in LLM-as-judge evaluators, directly relevant since LLM judges are widely used to score RL-trained models.

papers.title: Style Over Substance: Evaluation Biases for Large Language Models
papers.authors: Minghao Wu, Alham Fikri Aji
papers.inclusion_reason: Empirical demonstration that human and LLM evaluators alike systematically favor stylistic fluency over factual correctness, a core evaluation-validity critique for RLHF-tuned model assessment.

papers.title: Efficient Exploration for LLMs
papers.authors: Vikranth Dwaracherla, Seyed Mohammad Asghari, Botao Hao, Benjamin Van Roy
papers.inclusion_reason: Directly addresses the exploration problem in RLHF feedback collection, showing active/Thompson-sampling exploration sharply reduces the human feedback needed.

papers.title: Hindsight Credit Assignment
papers.authors: Anna Harutyunyan, Will Dabney, Thomas Mesnard, Mohammad Gheshlaghi Azar, Bilal Piot, Nicolas Heess, Hado P. van Hasselt, Gregory Wayne, Satinder Singh, Doina Precup, Remi Munos
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational credit-assignment theory for sparse/delayed rewards, providing the hindsight-distribution formalism that later work adapts for sparse-reward LLM RL training.

papers.title: RUDDER: Return Decomposition for Delayed Rewards
papers.authors: Jose A. Arjona-Medina, Michael Gillhofer, Michael Widrich, Thomas Unterthiner, Johannes Brandstetter, Sepp Hochreiter
papers.inclusion_reason: Canonical credit-assignment method for delayed/sparse rewards via return decomposition, a key theoretical building block relevant to sparse verifiable rewards in RLVR settings.

papers.title: Mitigating the Alignment Tax of RLHF
papers.authors: Yong Lin, Hangyu Lin, Wei Xiong, Shizhe Diao, Jianmeng Liu, Jipeng Zhang, Rui Pan, Haoxiang Wang, Wenbin Hu, Hanning Zhang, Hanze Dong, Renjie Pi, Han Zhao, Nan Jiang, Heng Ji, Yuan Yao, Tong Zhang
papers.inclusion_reason: Systematic empirical study of the alignment-forgetting tradeoff (alignment tax) across many mitigation methods, directly relevant to the alignment-tax angle of this subarea.

papers.title: The Effects of Reward Misspecification: Mapping and Mitigating Misaligned Models
papers.authors: Alexander Pan, Kush Bhatia, Jacob Steinhardt
papers.inclusion_reason: Systematic study of reward hacking as a function of agent capability, identifying phase transitions where more capable policies sharply exploit reward misspecification -- a key theoretical/empirical limitation finding.

papers.title: Iterative Preference Learning from Human Feedback: Bridging Theory and Practice for RLHF under KL-Constraint
papers.authors: Wei Xiong, Hanze Dong, Chenlu Ye, Ziqi Wang, Han Zhong, Heng Ji, Nan Jiang, Tong Zhang
papers.inclusion_reason: Theoretical treatment of exploration in KL-regularized RLHF, identifying the lack of strategic exploration in offline PPO/DPO as a core weakness and proposing exploration-aware algorithms with finite-sample guarantees.

papers.title: Reward Model Ensembles Help Mitigate Overoptimization
papers.authors: Thomas Coste, Usman Anwar, Robert Kirk, David Krueger
papers.inclusion_reason: Direct empirical follow-up to reward-overoptimization scaling laws, quantifying how ensemble-based conservative optimization reduces (but does not eliminate) overoptimization under BoN and PPO.

papers.title: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
papers.authors: Dario Amodei, Chris Olah, Jacob Steinhardt, Paul Christiano, John Schulman, Dan Mané
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational taxonomy of reward hacking, safe exploration, and distributional shift that frames the theoretical vocabulary this subarea's later RLHF/RLVR critiques build on.

papers.title: Cheating Automatic LLM Benchmarks: Null Models Achieve High Win Rates
papers.authors: Xiaosen Zheng, Tianyu Pang, Chao Du, Qian Liu, Jing Jiang, Min Lin
papers.inclusion_reason: Devastating benchmark-validity critique showing content-free 'null model' outputs can top AlpacaEval/Arena-Hard/MT-Bench leaderboards, undermining the automatic benchmarks widely used to evaluate RL-tuned chat models.

papers.title: Is RLHF More Difficult than Standard RL? A Theoretical Perspective
papers.authors: Yuanhao Wang, Qinghua Liu, Chi Jin
papers.inclusion_reason: Formal theoretical analysis reducing preference-based RL to reward-based RL with provable guarantees, a central piece of RLHF theory addressing an open question about the fundamental difficulty of learning from preferences.

papers.title: Confronting Reward Model Overoptimization with Constrained RLHF
papers.authors: Ted Moskovitz, Aaditya K. Singh, DJ Strouse, Tuomas Sandholm, Ruslan Salakhutdinov, Anca D. Dragan, Stephen McAleer
papers.inclusion_reason: Extends overoptimization analysis to the multi-reward-model composition setting, showing each component reward model has an effective-proxy region beyond which it should not be optimized further.

papers.title: Human Feedback is not Gold Standard
papers.authors: Tom Hosking, Phil Blunsom, Max Bartolo
papers.inclusion_reason: Critically examines the human preference judgments that underlie RLHF training and evaluation, showing confounders like assertiveness skew perceived quality -- a fundamental validity critique of the RLHF feedback signal itself.

papers.title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment
papers.authors: Amanda Askell, Yuntao Bai, Anna Chen, Dawn Drain, Deep Ganguli, Tom Henighan, Andy Jones, Nicholas Joseph, Ben Mann, Nova DasSarma, Nelson Elhage, Zac Hatfield-Dodds, Danny Hernandez, Jackson Kernion, Kamal Ndousse, Catherine Olsson, Dario Amodei, Tom Brown, Jack Clark, Sam McCandlish, Chris Olah, Jared Kaplan
papers.inclusion_reason: Early empirical framework for measuring alignment interventions' capability cost, an early data point for the alignment-tax / capability-tradeoff strand of this subarea.

papers.title: OpenRLHF: An Easy-to-use, Scalable and High-performance RLHF Framework
papers.authors: Jian Hu, Xibin Wu, Wei Shen, Jason Klein Liu, Zilin Zhu, Weixun Wang, Songlin Jiang, Haoran Wang, Hao Chen, Bin Chen, Weikai Fang, Xianyu, Yu Cao, Haotian Xu, Yiming Liu
papers.inclusion_reason: Widely-adopted open-source RLHF toolkit built on Ray/vLLM/DeepSpeed that re-designs model scheduling to remove the four-model co-location bottleneck, a canonical example of an open-source RLHF post-training toolkit.

papers.title: HybridFlow: A Flexible and Efficient RLHF Framework
papers.authors: Guangming Sheng, Chi Zhang, Zilingfeng Ye, Xibin Wu, Wang Zhang, Ru Zhang, Yanghua Peng, Haibin Lin, Chuan Wu
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces the hybrid single-/multi-controller programming model and 3D-HybridEngine underlying verl, one of the most widely used distributed RLHF training frameworks, with 1.5x-20.5x throughput gains over prior systems.

papers.title: ReaL: Efficient RLHF Training of Large Language Models with Parameter Reallocation
papers.authors: Zhiyu Mei, Wei Fu, Kaiwei Li, Guangju Wang, Huanchen Zhang, Yi Wu
papers.inclusion_reason: Proposes dynamic parameter reallocation across the training cluster to adapt parallelization strategy per RLHF stage, an automated-execution-plan-search system distinct from static placement schemes, with 2.0-10.6x speedups.

papers.title: DeepSpeed-Chat: Easy, Fast and Affordable RLHF Training of ChatGPT-like Models at All Scales
papers.authors: Zhewei Yao, Reza Yazdani Aminabadi, Olatunji Ruwase, Samyam Rajbhandari, Xiaoxia Wu, Ammar Ahmad Awan, Jeff Rasley, Minjia Zhang, Conglong Li, Connor Holmes, Zhongzhu Zhou, Michael Wyatt, Molly Smith, Lev Kurilenko, Heyang Qin, Masahiro Tanaka, Shuai Che, Shuaiwen Leon Song, Yuxiong He
papers.inclusion_reason: One of the earliest end-to-end open-source RLHF systems (built on DeepSpeed), establishing the co-located four-model training pattern that later infrastructure papers explicitly optimize against.

papers.title: NeMo-Aligner: Scalable Toolkit for Efficient Model Alignment
papers.authors: Gerald Shen, Zhilin Wang, Olivier Delalleau, Jiaqi Zeng, Yi Dong, Daniel Egert, Shengyang Sun, Jimmy Zhang, Sahil Jain, Ali Taghibakhshi, Markel Sanz Ausin, Ashwath Aithal, Oleksii Kuchaiev
papers.inclusion_reason: Production-grade alignment toolkit scaling RLHF/DPO/SteerLM/SPIN to thousands of GPUs and 340B+ parameter models via tensor/pipeline/data parallelism, representing the industrial open-source-toolkit end of the spectrum.

papers.title: Asynchronous RLHF: Faster and More Efficient Off-Policy RL for Language Models
papers.authors: Michael Noukhovitch, Shengyi Huang, Sophie Xhonneux, Arian Hosseini, Rishabh Agarwal, Aaron Courville
papers.inclusion_reason: Separates generation and learning to enable asynchronous off-policy RLHF, directly studying the online-vs-offline/on-policy-vs-off-policy tradeoff and finding online DPO most robust to staleness.

papers.title: Bridging Offline and Online Reinforcement Learning for LLMs
papers.authors: Jack Lanchantin, Angelica Chen, Janice Lan, Xian Li, Swarnadeep Saha, Tianlu Wang, Jing Xu, Ping Yu, Weizhe Yuan, Jason E Weston, Sainbayar Sukhbaatar, Ilia Kulikov
papers.inclusion_reason: Systematic empirical comparison of offline, semi-online, and online DPO/GRPO variants for LLM post-training, providing the clearest practical evidence on the online-vs-offline RL tradeoff named in the search angle.

papers.title: PipelineRL: Faster On-policy Reinforcement Learning for Long Sequence Generation
papers.authors: Alexandre Piché, Ehsan Kamalloo, Rafael Pardinas, Xiaoyin Chen, Dzmitry Bahdanau
papers.inclusion_reason: Concurrent generation/training pipeline with in-flight weight updates that keeps on-policy data while achieving ~2x speedups on 128 H100s, targeting the long-sequence-generation efficiency bottleneck in RL post-training.

papers.title: Prefix Grouper: Efficient GRPO Training through Shared-Prefix Forward
papers.authors: Zikang Liu, Tongtian Yue, Yepeng Tang, Longteng Guo, Junxian Cai, Qingbin Liu, Xi Chen, Jing Liu
papers.inclusion_reason: Restructures self-attention to encode a shared prompt prefix once across a GRPO group instead of redundantly per sample, a plug-and-play compute-efficient GRPO implementation technique with identical gradients to standard GRPO.

papers.title: Optimizing RLHF Training for Large Language Models with Stage Fusion
papers.authors: Yinmin Zhong, Zili Zhang, Bingyang Wu, Shengyu Liu, Yukun Chen, Changyi Wan, Hanpeng Hu, Lei Xia, Ranchen Ming, Yibo Zhu, Xin Jin
papers.inclusion_reason: Splits generation and training stages into finer-grained subtasks for inter- and intra-stage fusion, addressing GPU-utilization loss from generation-length skew and pipeline bubbles, with up to 3.7x throughput gains.

papers.title: StreamRL: Scalable, Heterogeneous, and Elastic RL for LLMs with Disaggregated Stream Generation
papers.authors: Yinmin Zhong, Zili Zhang, Xiaoniu Song, Hanpeng Hu, Chao Jin, Bingyang Wu, Nuo Chen, Yukun Chen, Yu Zhou, Changyi Wan, Hongyu Zhou, Yimin Jiang, Yibo Zhu, Daxin Jiang
papers.inclusion_reason: Argues disaggregated (rather than colocated) generation/training architecture scales better and supports heterogeneous, cross-datacenter RL post-training deployments, with 2.66x throughput improvement.

papers.title: AReaL: A Large-Scale Asynchronous Reinforcement Learning System for Language Reasoning
papers.authors: Wei Fu, Jiaxuan Gao, Xujie Shen, Chen Zhu, Zhiyu Mei, Chuyi He, Shusheng Xu, Guo Wei, Jun Mei, Jiashu Wang, Tongkai Yang, Binhang Yuan, Yi Wu
papers.inclusion_reason: Fully asynchronous RL system decoupling rollout generation from training with staleness-aware training and a decoupled PPO objective, a widely cited open-source system for large-scale reasoning-RL post-training.

papers.title: LlamaRL: A Distributed Asynchronous Reinforcement Learning Framework for Efficient Large-scale LLM Training
papers.authors: Bo Wu, Sid Wang, Yunhao Tang, Jia Ding, Eryk Helenowski, Liang Tan, Tengyu Xu, Tushar Gowda, Zhengxing Chen, Chen Zhu, Xiaocheng Tang, Yundi Qian, Beibei Zhu, Rui Hou
papers.inclusion_reason: Single-controller, native-PyTorch asynchronous RL framework validated at 8B/70B/405B parameter scale on thousand-plus-GPU clusters, reporting up to 10.7x speedup over DeepSpeed-Chat-style systems.

papers.title: An Adaptive Placement and Parallelism Framework for Accelerating RLHF Training
papers.authors: Youshao Xiao, Zhenglei Zhou, Fagui Mao, Weichang Wu, Shangchun Zhao, Lin Ju, Lei Liang, Xiaolu Zhang, Jun Zhou
papers.inclusion_reason: Early identification that fixed co-located model placement in RLHF training is suboptimal, proposing interleaving and disaggregated placement strategies with up to 11x improvement, predating and motivating later disaggregation systems.

papers.title: DistFlow: A Fully Distributed RL Framework for Scalable and Efficient LLM Post-Training
papers.authors: Zhixin Wang, Jiaming Xu, Tianyi Zhou, Mingjun Zhang, Liming Liu, Jiarui Hu, Dian Yang, Tongyu Wang, Ping Zhang, Jinlong Hou, Siyuan Feng, Yuan Qi, Yuan Cheng
papers.inclusion_reason: Multi-controller, DAG-scheduled RL framework achieving near-linear scaling to 512 GPUs and explicitly reporting growing throughput advantage at longer context lengths (8k to 64k), directly evidencing long-context RL training infrastructure gains.

papers.title: Trajectory Balance with Asynchrony: Decoupling Exploration and Learning for Fast, Scalable LLM Post-Training
papers.authors: Brian Bartoldson, Siddarth Venkatraman, James Diffenderfer, Moksh Jain, Tal Ben-Nun, Seanie Lee, Minsu Kim, Johan Obando-Ceron, Yoshua Bengio, Bhavya Kailkhura
papers.inclusion_reason: Uses an off-policy trajectory-balance objective with decoupled searcher/trainer nodes and a replay buffer to scale RL post-training compute, reporting 4x+ wall-clock speedups over online DPO baselines.

papers.title: Nested-ReFT: Efficient Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Model Fine-Tuning via Off-Policy Rollouts
papers.authors: Maxime Heuillet, Yufei Cui, Boxing Chen, Audrey Durand, Prasanna Parthasarathi
papers.inclusion_reason: Uses a dynamically layer-skipped subset of the target model as an off-policy behavior model to cut rollout inference cost during reinforced fine-tuning, a sample/compute-efficient RL technique with unbiased gradient guarantees.

papers.title: APRIL: Active Partial Rollouts in Reinforcement Learning to Tame Long-tail Generation
papers.authors: Yuzhen Zhou, Jiajun Li, Yusheng Su, Gowtham Ramesh, Zilin Zhu, Xiang Long, Chenyang Zhao, Jin Pan, Xiaodong Yu, Ze Wang, Kangrui Du, Jialian Wu, Ximeng Sun, Jiang Liu, Qiaolin Yu, Hao Chen, Zicheng Liu, Emad Barsoum
papers.inclusion_reason: System-level technique that proactively over-provisions and truncates rollouts to eliminate long-tail-response stalls during RL training, already integrated into the SLiME framework, improving rollout throughput 22.5% on average.

papers.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
papers.authors: Paul F. Christiano, Jan Leike, Tom B. Brown, Miljan Martic, Shane Legg, Dario Amodei
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational learned-reward-from-preferences algorithm that RLHF for LLMs directly descends from; establishes the reward-model-as-proxy paradigm this subarea studies.

papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
papers.authors: Yuntao Bai, Saurav Kadavath, Sandipan Kundu, Amanda Askell, Jackson Kernion, Andy Jones, Anna Chen, Anna Goldie, Azalia Mirhoseini, Cameron McKinnon, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces Constitutional AI and RL from AI Feedback (RLAIF), replacing human preference labels with AI-generated preferences under a set of principles -- the founding paper for the RLAIF branch of this subarea.

papers.title: RLAIF vs. RLHF: Scaling Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with AI Feedback
papers.authors: Harrison Lee, Samrat Phatale, Hassan Mansoor, Thomas Mesnard, Johan Ferret, Kellie Lu, Colton Bishop, Ethan Hall, Victor Carbune, Abhinav Rastogi, Sushant Prakash
papers.inclusion_reason: Large-scale empirical comparison of RLAIF vs RLHF across summarization/dialogue tasks, plus direct-RLAIF, showing off-the-shelf LLM feedback can match human-labeled reward models.

papers.title: Let's Verify Step by Step
papers.authors: Hunter Lightman, Vineet Kosaraju, Yura Burda, Harri Edwards, Bowen Baker, Teddy Lee, Jan Leike, John Schulman, Ilya Sutskever, Karl Cobbe
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces process reward models (PRMs) with step-level human supervision (PRM800K) and shows process supervision beats outcome supervision on MATH -- the founding PRM paper.

papers.title: Self-Rewarding Language Models
papers.authors: Weizhe Yuan, Richard Yuanzhe Pang, Kyunghyun Cho, Sainbayar Sukhbaatar, Jing Xu, Jason Weston
papers.inclusion_reason: Uses the policy model itself as an LLM-as-a-Judge generative reward model in an iterative DPO loop, showing both instruction-following and self-reward quality improve together -- key generative-reward-model / LLM-as-judge reference.

papers.title: Secrets of RLHF in Large Language Models Part II: Reward Modeling
papers.authors: Binghai Wang, Rui Zheng, Lu Chen, Yan Liu, Shihan Dou, Caishuang Huang, Wei Shen, Senjie Jin, Enyu Zhou, Chenyu Shi, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: Systematic study of reward-model training pathologies -- ambiguous preference pairs, OOD generalization failure in iterated RLHF -- with a measured-preference-strength and contrastive-learning fix, and meta-learning for iterative RLHF robustness.

papers.title: Interpretable Preferences via Multi-Objective Reward Modeling and Mixture-of-Experts
papers.authors: Haoxiang Wang, Wei Xiong, Tengyang Xie, Han Zhao, Tong Zhang
papers.inclusion_reason: ArmoRM: multi-objective absolute-rating reward model with a mixture-of-experts gate over interpretable objectives (honesty, verbosity, safety, ...), state-of-the-art on RewardBench and explicitly motivated by preventing reward hacking.

papers.title: HelpSteer2: Open-source dataset for training top-performing reward models
papers.authors: Zhilin Wang, Yi Dong, Olivier Delalleau, Jiaqi Zeng, Gerald Shen, Daniel Egert, Jimmy J. Zhang, Makesh Narsimhan Sreedhar, Oleksii Kuchaiev
papers.inclusion_reason: Open, multi-attribute (helpfulness/correctness/coherence/complexity/verbosity) human preference dataset behind the Nemotron-4 340B reward model, a key open resource for reward-model design and calibration research.

papers.title: WARM: On the Benefits of Weight Averaged Reward Models
papers.authors: Alexandre Ramé, Nino Vieillard, Léonard Hussenot, Robert Dadashi, Geoffrey Cideron, Olivier Bachem, Johan Ferret
papers.inclusion_reason: Weight-space averaging of multiple fine-tuned reward models to mitigate reward hacking under distribution shift and preference-label inconsistency, cheaper than prediction ensembling.

papers.title: ODIN: Disentangled Reward Mitigates Hacking in RLHF
papers.authors: Lichang Chen, Chen Zhu, Davit Soselia, Jiuhai Chen, Tianyi Zhou, Tom Goldstein, Heng Huang, Mohammad Shoeybi, Bryan Catanzaro
papers.inclusion_reason: Directly targets length-hacking by disentangling a length-invariant reward component from a length component during RM training, with PPO ablations showing genuine quality gains rather than length inflation.

papers.title: One Token to Fool LLM-as-a-Judge
papers.authors: Yulai Zhao, Haolin Liu, Dian Yu, Sunyuan Kung, Meijia Chen, Haitao Mi, Dong Yu
papers.inclusion_reason: Shows generative reward models / LLM-as-judge verifiers can be fooled by single non-word tokens or generic reasoning openers into awarding false-positive rewards -- a sharp, recent robustness failure case for generative reward models.

papers.title: Generative Reward Models
papers.authors: Dakota Mahan, Duy Van Phung, Rafael Rafailov, Chase Blagden, Nathan Lile, Louis Castricato, Jan-Philipp Fränken, Chelsea Finn, Alon Albalak
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces GenRM, an iterative algorithm training an LLM on self-generated reasoning traces to produce synthetic preference labels that better match human judgments than classic scalar RMs or vanilla LLM-as-judge.

papers.title: Taming Overconfidence in LLMs: Reward Calibration in RLHF
papers.authors: Jixuan Leng, Chengsong Huang, Banghua Zhu, Jiaxin Huang
papers.inclusion_reason: Identifies verbalized-confidence miscalibration induced by standard RLHF reward models and proposes calibrated reward shaping (PPO-M / PPO-C) -- direct evidence for reward-model calibration as its own axis of this subarea.

papers.title: STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning
papers.authors: Eric Zelikman, Yuhuai Wu, Jesse Mu, Noah D. Goodman
papers.inclusion_reason: Founding paper of the self-taught-reasoner lineage: bootstraps rationale generation by fine-tuning only on rationales that led to correct answers, the direct ancestor of later outcome-reward RL-for-reasoning methods.

papers.title: Reinforced Self-Training (ReST) for Language Modeling
papers.authors: Caglar Gulcehre, Tom Le Paine, Srivatsan Srinivasan, Ksenia Konyushkova, Lotte Weerts, Abhishek Sharma, Aditya Siddhant, Alex Ahern, Miaosen Wang, Chenjie Gu, Wolfgang Macherey, Arnaud Doucet, Orhan Firat, Nando de Freitas
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces the Grow/Improve offline-RL self-training loop that ReST-EM and later reasoning-RL pipelines build on, bridging STaR-style bootstrapping and full RLHF-style optimization.

papers.title: Beyond Human Data: Scaling Self-Training for Problem-Solving with Language Models
papers.authors: Avi Singh, John D. Co-Reyes, Rishabh Agarwal, Ankesh Anand, Piyush Patil, Xavier Garcia, Peter J. Liu, James Harrison, Jaehoon Lee, Kelvin Xu, Aaron Parisi, Abhishek Kumar, Alex Alemi, Alex Rizkowsky, Azade Nova, Ben Adlam, Bernd Bohnet, Gamaleldin Elsayed, Hanie Sedghi, Igor Mordatch, Isabelle Simpson, Izzeddin Gur, Jasper Snoek, Jeffrey Pennington, Jiri Hron, Kathleen Kenealy, Kevin Swersky, Kshiteej Mahajan, Laura Culp, Lechao Xiao, Maxwell L. Bileschi, Noah Constant, Roman Novak, Rosanne Liu, Tris Warkentin, Yundi Qian, Yamini Bansal, Ethan Dyer, Behnam Neyshabur, Jascha Sohl-Dickstein, Noah Fiedel
papers.inclusion_reason: ReST-EM: applies expectation-maximization self-training with a binary correctness (verifiable) reward on math and code, showing self-generated data beats human data at scale -- a key rung in the STaR lineage.

papers.title: Quiet-STaR: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Think Before Speaking
papers.authors: Eric Zelikman, Georges Harik, Yijia Shao, Varuna Jayasiri, Nick Haber, Noah D. Goodman
papers.inclusion_reason: Generalizes STaR from task-specific rationale bootstrapping to token-level rationale generation via REINFORCE, extending the self-taught-reasoner lineage toward general pretraining-time reasoning.

papers.title: Math-Shepherd: Verify and Reinforce LLMs Step-by-step without Human Annotations
papers.authors: Peiyi Wang, Lei Li, Zhihong Shao, R.X. Xu, Damai Dai, Yifei Li, Deli Chen, Y.Wu, Zhifang Sui
papers.inclusion_reason: Automates process-reward-model construction (no human step labels) and uses it for step-by-step PPO reinforcement, a key precursor to DeepSeekMath's GRPO work by an overlapping author team.

papers.title: Improve Mathematical Reasoning in Language Models by Automated Process Supervision
papers.authors: Liangchen Luo, Yinxiao Liu, Rosanne Liu, Samrat Phatale, Meiqi Guo, Harsh Lara, Yunxuan Li, Lei Shu, Yun Zhu, Lei Meng, Jiao Sun, Abhinav Rastogi
papers.inclusion_reason: OmegaPRM: divide-and-conquer MCTS for automated process-supervision label generation at scale (1.5M+ annotations), representative of the Google DeepMind line of automated PRM construction for reasoning RL.

papers.title: O1 Replication Journey: A Strategic Progress Report -- Part 1
papers.authors: Yiwei Qin, Xuefeng Li, Haoyang Zou, Yixiu Liu, Shijie Xia, Zhen Huang, Yixin Ye, Weizhe Yuan, Hector Liu, Yuanzhi Li, Pengfei Liu
papers.inclusion_reason: First open, journal-style replication effort for OpenAI o1's reasoning training pipeline, documenting the community's early hypotheses about o1's RL recipe before DeepSeek-R1's release.

papers.title: Tulu 3: Pushing Frontiers in Open Language Model Post-Training
papers.authors: Nathan Lambert, Jacob Morrison, Valentina Pyatkin, Shengyi Huang, Hamish Ivison, Faeze Brahman, Lester James V. Miranda, Alisa Liu, Nouha Dziri, Shane Lyu, Yuling Gu, Saumya Malik, Victoria Graf, Jena D. Hwang, Jiangjiang Yang, Ronan Le Bras, Oyvind Tafjord, Chris Wilhelm, Luca Soldaini, Noah A. Smith, Yizhong Wang, Pradeep Dasigi, Hannaneh Hajishirzi
papers.inclusion_reason: Coins and formalizes Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), replacing the RLHF reward model with a binary correctness verifier -- the term-defining paper for this subarea's core method.

papers.title: OpenAI o1 System Card
papers.authors: OpenAI
papers.inclusion_reason: The primary citable technical disclosure of o1's large-scale RL-on-chain-of-thought training approach, the closed-model counterpart the open replications (R1, Kimi k1.5, o1-Journey) are responding to.

papers.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: DeepSeek-AI: Daya Guo, Dejian Yang, Haowei Zhang, Junxiao Song, Ruoyu Zhang, Runxin Xu, Qihao Zhu, Shirong Ma, Peiyi Wang, Xiao Bi, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: The central paper of this subarea: R1-Zero shows pure GRPO-based RLVR (no SFT) elicits emergent long chain-of-thought reasoning, and R1 adds cold-start/multi-stage training to match o1-level performance.

papers.title: Kimi k1.5: Scaling Reinforcement Learning with LLMs
papers.authors: Kimi Team (Angang Du, Bofei Gao, Bowei Xing, et al.)
papers.inclusion_reason: Contemporaneous large-scale industrial RL-for-reasoning report that deliberately avoids MCTS/value functions/PRMs in favor of a simplified RL framework, an important comparison point to DeepSeek-R1's recipe.

papers.title: rStar-Math: Small LLMs Can Master Math Reasoning with Self-Evolved Deep Thinking
papers.authors: Xinyu Guan, Li Lyna Zhang, Jiahang Xu, Chengruidong Zhang, Ning Shang, Fan Yang, Mao Yang
papers.inclusion_reason: Combines MCTS-based code-verified rollouts with a process preference model to self-evolve small models to o1-level math reasoning without distillation, a process-reward-centric counterpoint to R1's outcome-only recipe.

papers.title: Process Reinforcement through Implicit Rewards
papers.authors: Ganqu Cui, Lifan Yuan, Zefan Wang, Hanbin Wang, Wendi Li, Bingxiang He, Yuchen Fan, Tianyu Yu, Qixin Xu, Weize Chen, Jiarui Yuan, Huayu Chen, Kaiyan Zhang, Xingtai Lv, Shuo Wang, Yuan Yao, Xu Han, Hao Peng, Yu Cheng, Zhiyuan Liu, Maosong Sun, Bowen Zhou, Ning Ding
papers.inclusion_reason: PRIME derives dense process rewards implicitly from an outcome-trained reward model during online RL, closing much of the outcome-vs-process reward gap without the cost of explicit step annotation.

papers.title: Open-Reasoner-Zero: An Open Source Approach to Scaling Up Reinforcement Learning on the Base Model
papers.authors: Jingcheng Hu, Yinmin Zhang, Qi Han, Daxin Jiang, Xiangyu Zhang, Heung-Yeung Shum
papers.inclusion_reason: Shows vanilla PPO with plain rule-based rewards (no GRPO, no KL penalty) can replicate R1-Zero-style scaling more efficiently, an important minimalist counterpoint on which RL-algorithm ingredients actually matter.

papers.title: SimpleRL-Zoo: Investigating and Taming Zero Reinforcement Learning for Open Base Models in the Wild
papers.authors: Weihao Zeng, Yuzhen Huang, Qian Liu, Wei Liu, Keqing He, Zejun Ma, Junxian He
papers.inclusion_reason: Systematically replicates zero-RL training (R1-Zero paradigm) across 10 base model families beyond Qwen, isolating which base-model properties and reward-design choices are necessary for the 'aha moment' to emerge.

papers.title: Absolute Zero: Reinforced Self-play Reasoning with Zero Data
papers.authors: Andrew Zhao, Yiran Wu, Yang Yue, Tong Wu, Quentin Xu, Yang Yue, Matthieu Lin, Shenzhi Wang, Qingyun Wu, Zilong Zheng, Gao Huang
papers.inclusion_reason: Pushes RLVR to its logical extreme: a single model proposes and solves its own verifiable tasks via a code executor with zero external data, testing how far outcome-verifiable RL can go without any human curation.

papers.title: Recursively Summarizing Books with Human Feedback
papers.authors: Jeff Wu, Long Ouyang, Daniel M. Ziegler, Nisan Stiennon, Ryan Lowe, Jan Leike, Paul Christiano
papers.inclusion_reason: Extends the learning-to-summarize RLHF lineage to scalable oversight via task decomposition, bridging RLHF and scalable-oversight research.

papers.title: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
papers.authors: Reiichiro Nakano, Jacob Hilton, Suchir Balaji, Jeff Wu, Long Ouyang, Christina Kim, Christopher Hesse, Shantanu Jain, Vineet Kosaraju, William Saunders, Xu Jiang, Karl Cobbe, Tyna Eloundou, Gretchen Krueger, Kevin Button, Matthew Knight, Benjamin Chess, John Schulman
papers.inclusion_reason: Extends RLHF (imitation learning plus reward modeling plus RL) to a tool-use/browsing setting, a major branch of the InstructGPT-era RLHF lineage.

papers.title: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
papers.authors: Amelia Glaese, Nat McAleese, Maja Trebacz, John Aslanides, Vlad Firoiu, Timo Ewalds, Maribeth Rauh, Laura Weidinger, Martin Chadwick, Phoebe Thacker, Lucy Campbell-Gillingham, Jonathan Uesato, Po-Sen Huang, Ramona Comanescu, Fan Yang, Abigail See, Sumanth Dathathri, Rory Greig, Charlie Chen, Doug Fritz, Jaume Sanchez Elias, Richard Green, Sona Mokra, Nicholas Fernando, Boxi Wu, Rachel Foley, Susannah Young, Iason Gabriel, William Isaac, John Mellor, Demis Hassabis, Koray Kavukcuoglu, Lisa Anne Hendricks, Geoffrey Irving
papers.inclusion_reason: DeepMind's Sparrow: RLHF applied to dialogue with rule-conditional reward models, a major foundational RLHF-for-alignment system contemporaneous with InstructGPT.

papers.title: Teaching language models to support answers with verified quotes
papers.authors: Jacob Menick, Maja Trebacz, Vladimir Mikulik, John Aslanides, Francis Song, Martin Chadwick, Mia Glaese, Susannah Young, Lucy Campbell-Gillingham, Geoffrey Irving, Nat McAleese
papers.inclusion_reason: GopherCite: RLHF (reinforcement learning from human preferences) applied to train citation-grounded QA, an important early RLHF-for-LLMs application from DeepMind.

papers.title: Interactively Shaping Agents via Human Reinforcement: The TAMER Framework
papers.authors: W. Bradley Knox, Peter Stone
papers.venue: Proceedings of the Fifth International Conference on Knowledge Capture (K-CAP 2009)
papers.inclusion_reason: The TAMER framework is the earliest widely-cited foundation for learning policies directly from real-time human evaluative feedback, predating and directly informing deep-RL-from-human-preferences work.

papers.title: Deep TAMER: Interactive Agent Shaping in High-Dimensional State Spaces
papers.authors: Garrett Warnell, Nicholas Waytowich, Vernon Lawhern, Peter Stone
papers.inclusion_reason: Extends TAMER's human-feedback shaping to deep neural network policies in high-dimensional state spaces, a key stepping stone toward deep-RL-from-human-feedback methods.

papers.title: Interactive Learning from Policy-Dependent Human Feedback
papers.authors: James MacGlashan, Mark K. Ho, Robert Loftin, Bei Peng, Guan Wang, David L. Roberts, Matthew E. Taylor, Michael L. Littman
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces COACH, showing that human feedback is policy-dependent (advantage-like) rather than a stationary reward, a foundational insight for interactive human-feedback RL that predates LLM RLHF.

papers.title: APRIL: Active Preference-learning based Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Riad Akrour, Marc Schoenauer, Michele Sebag
papers.inclusion_reason: Early foundational preference-based RL work using trajectory-ranking feedback instead of scalar rewards, directly anticipating the pairwise-comparison reward modeling used throughout LLM RLHF.

papers.title: Is Reinforcement Learning (Not) for Natural Language Processing: Benchmarks, Baselines, and Building Blocks for Natural Language Policy Optimization
papers.authors: Rajkumar Ramamurthy, Prithviraj Ammanabrolu, Kiante Brantley, Jack Hessel, Rafet Sifa, Christian Bauckhage, Hannaneh Hajishirzi, Yejin Choi
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces RL4LMs, an open-source RL-for-LM-alignment library and GRUE benchmark, and systematically documents RLHF training-instability challenges -- foundational infrastructure and empirical grounding for the field.

papers.title: Offline RL for Natural Language Generation with Implicit Language Q-Learning
papers.authors: Charlie Snell, Ilya Kostrikov, Yi Su, Mengjiao Yang, Sergey Levine
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational offline-RL alternative to PPO-based RLHF for language models, establishing value-based (rather than policy-gradient) reward optimization as a viable branch of the same technique lineage.
